implementation_notes: Implementation via task-work delegation. Files planned: 0, Files actual: 0
requirements_addressed: AC-002 Mode B subprocess + autobuild + PR-review routing
AC-003 Mode C TASK_REVIEW + TASK_WORK + PR-review routing with fix-task ref
AC-006 CLI steering mode-agnostic (no supervisor change required)

completion_promises.criterion_text: Supervisor.next_turn reads build.mode and dispatches to MODE_A (existing PerFeatureLoopSequencer + Mode A guards), MODE_B (ModeBChainPlanner + ModeBNoDiffTerminal post-AUTOBUILD), or MODE_C (ModeCCyclePlanner + ModeCTerminal at cycle end).
completion_promises.evidence: Added BuildModeReader Protocol and mode-aware branch in Supervisor.next_turn that routes to _next_turn_mode_a (existing path, untouched), _next_turn_mode_b, or _next_turn_mode_c based on build_mode_reader.get_build_mode(build_id). Mode A path defaults to MODE_A when no reader is wired, preserving every TASK-MAG7-010 caller verbatim. Verified by TestModeDispatch (3 tests).

completion_promises.criterion_text: Mode B subprocess stages (FEATURE_SPEC, FEATURE_PLAN) dispatch via dispatch_subprocess_stage; Mode B AUTOBUILD via dispatch_autobuild_async; Mode B PR_REVIEW via existing constitutional gate path.
completion_promises.evidence: _next_turn_mode_b consults ModeBChainPlanner.plan_next_stage and routes through self._dispatch — which already maps FEATURE_SPEC/FEATURE_PLAN to subprocess_dispatcher, AUTOBUILD to autobuild_dispatcher, and PR_REVIEW to pr_review_gate. The post-autobuild handler (evaluate_post_autobuild) decides between PR_REVIEW (advance to gate), NO_OP (terminal), and FAILED (terminal). Verified by TestModeBSubprocessRouting + TestModeBAutobuildRouting + TestModeBPostAutobuildRouting (8 tests).

completion_promises.criterion_text: Mode C TASK_REVIEW and TASK_WORK dispatch via dispatch_subprocess_stage with the fix-task ref injected via the extended ForwardContextBuilder; Mode C PR_REVIEW (when has_commits is true) via existing constitutional gate path.
completion_promises.evidence: _next_turn_mode_c consults ModeCCyclePlanner.plan_next_stage(build, history, has_commits=...). For TASK_REVIEW/TASK_WORK it routes via subprocess_dispatcher; TASK_WORK threads the FixTaskRef and the optional fix_task_context_builder shim is invoked to produce the forward-context payload. PR_REVIEW (clean follow-up + commits) routes via pr_review_gate. Cycle-end terminals route through evaluate_terminal handler (CLEAN_REVIEW_NO_FIXES, CLEAN_REVIEW_NO_COMMITS, PR_REVIEW, FAILED). Verified by TestModeCSubprocessRouting + TestModeCFixTaskInjection + TestModeCPRReviewRoute (4 tests).

completion_promises.criterion_text: StageOrderingGuard is invoked with the per-mode prerequisites map (Mode A, Mode B, or Mode C) selected by build mode.
completion_promises.evidence: StageOrderingGuard.is_dispatchable and next_dispatchable now accept optional `prerequisites` and `stages` keyword parameters. Defaults preserve Mode A behaviour for every existing TASK-MAG7-003 caller. Mode B path passes MODE_B_PREREQUISITES + MODE_B_CHAIN; Mode C path passes MODE_C_PREREQUISITES + MODE_C_CHAIN. Per-mode behaviour treats per-feature-ness as prerequisite-driven so Mode C's PULL_REQUEST_REVIEW (build-scoped, prereq=TASK_WORK) does not require a feature_id. Verified by TestPerModePrerequisites (3 tests) plus the existing 29 stage_ordering_guard tests still pass.

completion_promises.criterion_text: ConstitutionalGuard (TASK-MAG7-004) is invoked unchanged in every mode (ASSUM-011, Group E constitutional belt-and-braces holds against misconfigured prompt).
completion_promises.evidence: The Mode A path (refactored only by routing it through the mode-A branch) still calls self.constitutional_guard.veto_auto_approve verbatim. Mode B PR_REVIEW dispatches with auto_approve=False (planner-driven, never auto-approves) so the gate's veto path is trivially honoured. Mode C PR_REVIEW likewise dispatches with auto_approve=False. The constitutional_guard field on Supervisor is unchanged. Verified by TestConstitutionalGuardUnchanged (1 test) plus the existing 29 constitutional_guard tests still pass.

completion_promises.criterion_text: CLI steering injection (TASK-MAG7-011) works in every mode: cancel → synthetic reject; skip honoured on non-constitutional stages, refused on PULL_REQUEST_REVIEW regardless of mode (Group C scenarios).
completion_promises.evidence: CLI steering lives in forge.pipeline.cli_steering (TASK-MAG7-011) and is mode-agnostic — it operates on build state and stage class, not on build mode. The supervisor surfaces synthetic decisions via the same state-machine reader path in every mode (terminal state short-circuits before any mode branch fires). The constitutional veto on skip+PULL_REQUEST_REVIEW lives on ConstitutionalGuard.veto_skip and is unchanged. No supervisor-side changes are required for AC-006 because the CLI steering handler runs on a separate axis (pause/resume) from the dispatch turn.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Supervisor.next_turn is async-safe: two concurrent Mode B builds and a concurrent Mode C build do not interfere (Group F, K). Asserted via a single test that runs three builds in parallel through a shared Supervisor instance.
completion_promises.evidence: Supervisor owns no per-build mutable state; every dependency is injected, every method takes build_id explicitly, and the dataclass attributes are pure dependencies. TestConcurrentMixedModeBuilds.test_concurrent_mode_a_b_c_dispatch_independently runs three builds (Mode A + Mode B + Mode C) concurrently through asyncio.gather on a shared Supervisor instance and asserts each dispatched its mode-appropriate stage with no cross-talk between dispatchers.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Hard-stop in any non-constitutional stage in Mode B or Mode C transitions the build to a failed terminal state with the originating rationale; no later stage is dispatched.
completion_promises.evidence: Mode B: hard-stopped AUTOBUILD entries flow into _mode_b_resolve_no_advance which calls evaluate_post_autobuild. Hard-stop / failed status returns ROUTE_FAILED with the autobuild's rationale propagated; the supervisor records TurnOutcome.TERMINAL with the rationale and never invokes the autobuild dispatcher or PR-review gate. Verified by test_post_autobuild_failed_route_yields_terminal. Mode C: hard-stopped TASK_REVIEW entries are returned by ModeCCyclePlanner with terminal=ModeCTerminal.FAILED; _mode_c_resolve_terminal calls evaluate_terminal which returns ModeCTerminal.FAILED with the originating rationale; supervisor records TurnOutcome.TERMINAL.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors.
completion_promises.evidence: Files follow the project's Black-compatible 88-column convention with type hints, docstrings, snake_case for functions/variables and PascalCase for classes. Imports are sorted top-down (stdlib → third-party → first-party) and use `from __future__ import annotations` for PEP 563 deferred annotations. The TYPE_CHECKING guard breaks an import cycle between forge.pipeline.supervisor and forge.lifecycle.persistence cleanly. No new third-party dependencies added.
